title: Oscars 2026: Who Will Win, Who Should Win
text: This Rolling Stone feature examines the competitive 2026 Oscar race, highlighting a tight battle between two films: Paul Thomas Anderson's One Battle After Another and Ryan Coogler's Sinners. Best Picture: The article describes this as a real barn burner with One Battle After Another previously favored, but Sinners experiencing a significant surge. The writer notes that both are landmark movies with a real chance of an upset here. Best Director: Anderson leads with wins at Golden Globes, Critics Choice, BAFTAs, and the Directors Guild Awards, though Coogler remains a strong contender. Best Actor: Michael B. Jordan is positioned as the frontrunner following his SAG Award win for Sinners, overtaking earlier momentum for Timothée Chalamet in Marty Supreme. Best Actress: Jessie Buckley appears nearly unbeatable for her role in Hamnet, having secured Golden Globe, BAFTA, and SAG victories. The article emphasizes that any year in which these two competing films vie for top honors represents a good Oscars year, reflecting the quality of the 2025 film slate overall.
